canon.statement: Adding a package reference enables functionality; modules auto-register via IKoanAutoRegistrar — 'No manual service configuration.' Reaffirmed as load-bearing in the consolidation era: capability tokens must live in each pillar's own abstractions assembly so 'referencing Koan.Data.Vector is what should surface the vector tokens' (ARCH-0084 Forces #3), and 'Reference = Intent must keep working... zero per-app wiring' (ARCH-0086 Forces #4).
canon.source: docs/architecture/principles.md (Core Philosophy); README.md §2; docs/decisions/ARCH-0084 Forces; docs/decisions/ARCH-0086 Forces

canon.statement: 'Entities handle their own persistence. No repository pattern needed' — static Todo.Get(id)/Where(), instance todo.Save()/Delete(). Pitched as 'AR ergonomics... while neutralizing the common AR traps' (README) and 'entities own CRUD, orchestration offloaded' (comparison.md). The front-door facades (Entity<T>, Data<T,K>, Vector<T>, Job<T>, [Embedding]) are explicitly declared 'already coherent — do NOT churn' by the consolidation plan §3.

'Same code works across any storage backend... Storage backend becomes a deployment concern, not development concern.' Comparison.md: 'Truly storage-agnostic: same Entity<> code across PostgreSQL, MongoDB, SQLite, JSON/Redis, and vector stores.' Enterprise framing: 'Vendor lock-in risk → Provider transparency across data, messaging, AI.'
'SoC, KISS, YAGNI, DRY applied consistently... Small modules, explicit composition, clear naming.' One-line EntityController<Todo> vs manual CRUD boilerplate is the canonical illustration.
'Explicit config beats discovery. Fail fast on misconfig.' Four-layer hierarchy: provider defaults < appsettings < env vars < code overrides.

'Start simple, add complexity incrementally' (Level 1 entity → Level 2 business logic → Level 3 multi-provider). Overview.md: 'Everything starts minimal and grows by intent — add packages, not boilerplate'; 'Koan grows with your intent, not against it.'
canon.source: docs/architecture/principles.md (Design Principle 3); docs/getting-started/overview.md Stage 0 + closing line

canon.statement: 'Framework enhances but never constrains' — direct SQL when needed, custom controllers when needed; comparison.md scores Koan 'Direct SQL / custom controllers while preserving sets/caps.'

KoanEnv environment detection, automatic /api/health liveness/readiness, env-var config override; Strategic Direction: 'Position as premier choice for containerized .NET applications with sophisticated orchestration capabilities.'

'Framework self-documents what's configured' (boot reports, KoanEnv.DumpSnapshot, structured event IDs); CLAUDE.md: 'Services describe their capabilities in structured boot reports'; Strategic Direction: 'Emphasize BootReport observability and self-documenting systems as enterprise-grade operational benefits.'
canon.source: docs/architecture/principles.md (Observability + Operational Sophistication); CLAUDE.md Core Principles

canon.principle: Small teams → sophisticated apps (the value proposition)
canon.statement: 'The .NET framework that makes small teams capable of sophisticated solutions through intelligent automation, AI-native patterns, and elegant scaling' (README masthead); 'With Koan, enterprise ambition no longer demands enterprise headcount' (enterprise-adoption closing).

'Semantic search, embeddings, and streaming pipelines are first-class, not bolt-ons' (README); 'Make traditionally complex integrations (AI, OAuth, CQRS, event sourcing) accessible through sane defaults' (principles.md Complex Scenario Simplification).

canon.principle: Fewer but more meaningful parts / descaffold, don't consolidate
canon.statement: 'The framework so far is a viability exercise — now harden it for v1... remove the development scaffolding and leave the parts that work. Consolidation and deletion are the same move seen from two sides.' For every part: 'is this load-bearing for the proven product, or scaffolding from the proof-of-viability phase?' Scaffolding → cut, 'do not lovingly refactor it.'

'Drive down the ideas a developer must hold; internal plumbing may remain many small invisible parts.' Refined by the verified audit: the merge test is 'is this a distinct intent a developer would reference alone?' — keep tiny packages that are distinct intents (Json.Strict, Sse), merge internal seams.

'Collapse where ≥2 real usages prove the overlap. Resist the elegant abstraction no app needs (second-system effect).' 'Full solution + every dogfood sample + a doc-API lint, all green, or it doesn't merge.' 'A part earns its place only if a dogfood app reaches for it naturally or removing it forces ceremony back into an app.' Settled = 'a new dogfood app of a known shape can be built end-to-end without editing the framework.'

canon.principle: Unified capability model as the sole declaration→negotiation→report surface; fail-loud is canon
canon.statement: One Capability/CapabilitySet primitive replaces ~40 ad-hoc types; 'Fail-loud is canon (DATA-0097: an un-pushable filter node is a hard error, never silent narrowing). The unified model must preserve a single, loud not-supported path' (CapabilityNotSupportedException). Implemented and legacy deleted (Facet 1 COMPLETE).

canon.principle: KoanModule: one boot-time authoring unit, built over working machinery, never big-bang
canon.statement: 'One authoring surface (Id, DependsOn, Register, Start, Report)'; 'The machinery (source-gen discovery, topological ordering) works... replacing it is pure risk. Build over it'; migration is 'opportunistic and green-at-each', additive bridge keeps 85 registrars working. Notably evidence-corrected: 'capabilities are per-type/runtime (the provider), not per-assembly/boot (the module)' — the ADR explicitly corrects the plan's own sketch.
canon.source: docs/decisions/ARCH-0086 (Decision, Forces, granularity finding); CLAUDE.md (ARCH-0086 entry)

canon.principle: The premium-DX audience for internals is the adapter author
canon.statement: 'The premium-DX audience is the adapter author, not the app developer (samples never touch these types). Optimize for clean declaration + clean negotiation + free self-report.' One file → 'discovery, ordering, negotiation, self-report, health, all free' (plan §4.5).

'Every adapter, connector, coherence channel, and pillar core ships at least one integration spec that goes through real AddKoan() reflective discovery... Unit tests with fakes are insufficient — they structurally cannot reveal composition or shared-resource bugs.'

tensions.tension: 'Reference = Intent' vs an OTel-laden kernel: referencing Koan.Core (the mandatory base of every app, including console workers) expresses no telemetry intent, yet it hard-carries 5 OpenTelemetry packages including AspNetCore/Http/Runtime instrumentation and the OTLP exporter. By the consolidation plan's own §3a merge test ('a distinct intent a developer would reference alone'), observability is a distinct intent that should be its own package. principles.md compounds it by claiming telemetry is opt-in via AddKoan(options => options.EnableTelemetry = true) — AddKoan() takes no options lambda (src/Koan.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:23) and the only EnableTelemetry in src is on TransactionOptions. Runtime activation is conditional (Observability/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs disables in prod without OTLP endpoint), but the dependency weight is unconditional.
tensions.evidence: src/Koan.Core/Koan.Core.csproj (OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting, Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol, Instrumentation.AspNetCore/Http/Runtime 1.15.x); src/Koan.Core/Observability/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:15-32; principles.md 'Telemetry Integration (opt-in)' section

tensions.tension: 'Deterministic Configuration — fail fast on misconfig' (founding) AND 'fail-loud is canon' (ARCH-0084) vs the bootstrap silently swallowing every initializer exception: AppBootstrapper runs all module initializers inside catch { /* best-effort to avoid failing the host during bootstrap */ }, plus four more bare catch{} blocks in the same file (assembly refs, manifest reflection). A misbehaving module's DI registration vanishes without a trace — the exact 'silent narrowing' the capability model outlaws at query time is institutionalized at boot time. There is a real underlying conflict: Reference=Intent auto-boot wants resilience (one bad package shouldn't kill the host), fail-fast wants loudness — but the current blanket swallow resolves it maximally toward silence, contradicting both the principle doc and the consolidation-era canon.

'Simplicity First — YAGNI applied consistently' vs 110 src projects, ~40 capability types, 7 registration interfaces, 85 auto-registrars, and ~25 zero-consumer experimental projects (Stage 1). The framework's own plan concedes breadth is 'the chief liability' and that much of it is 'scaffolding... it existed to prove a capability, not because a real product needs it as a distinct part' — e.g. the 8 Koan.AI.{Compute,Training,Agents,Eval,Review,Models,Prompt,Orchestration} sub-pillars with a single sample consumer. This is the acknowledged self-tension the consolidation era exists to repay; Facets 0–2 are done, Facet 4 (descaffold/cut) — the one that actually deletes the YAGNI violations — is not started.
tensions.evidence: 110 csproj under src/ (counted); foundation-consolidation-plan.md §0 ('breadth... is the chief liability'), §3 ranked targets, §3a deletion candidates; principles.md Design Principle 1

tensions.tension: 'Escape Hatches Everywhere' vs the escape hatches as documented: principles.md's flagship example User.ExecuteSql("SELECT...") exists nowhere in src (zero grep hits), and the dedicated escape-hatch package Koan.Data.Direct has zero consumers repo-wide — no project, sample, or test references it; only its own 7 files mention it (it even got a KoanModule conversion in ARCH-0086 pass 1 while remaining unconsumed). The real hatches exist under different names (custom controllers, IRawQueryRepository per DATA-0096), but the advertised surface is fictional and the packaged hatch fails the framework's own dogfood-earns-its-place rule ('a part earns its place only if a dogfood app reaches for it naturally').
tensions.evidence: Grep 'ExecuteSql' → 0 hits in src; grep 'Data.Direct' across all csproj → 0 external references; principles.md Design Principle 4; foundation-consolidation-plan.md §1

tensions.tension: The marketing front door sells features that do not exist or ride the condemned stack, while never mentioning the settled core. Flow.OnUpdate<Todo>(...) is showcased in README §4, overview.md Stage 3, AND enterprise-adoption.md's security/compliance example — zero matches in any .cs file in the repo. 'koan export compose' (README §5, enterprise-adoption pilot step 3) lives only in the ARCH-0077-condemned Orchestration CLI. modules-overview.md describes Koan.Jobs.Core as 'register jobs via DI (IJob implementations) and configure job runner' — the pre-JOBS-0005 world; the actual entity-first IKoanJob<TSelf> system is undocumented there. Meanwhile README says nothing about the capability model, the Jobs capability ladder, the Cache coherence system, or Security.Trust — the parts Stage 1 found settled.
tensions.evidence: Grep 'Flow.OnUpdate|OnUpdate<' in **/*.cs → 0 files; README.md §4-5; docs/getting-started/overview.md Stage 3; enterprise-adoption.md §3; modules-overview.md §1 Jobs entry; ARCH-0077

tensions.tension: A framework whose flagship operational claim is self-describing infrastructure cannot describe itself: README badge, principles.md, CLAUDE.md, and every doc front-matter say v0.6.3; real version is 0.17.x via NBGV; public NuGet is stale at 0.8.x; package IDs publish as Sylin.Koan.* but README/overview quickstarts instruct 'dotnet add package Koan.Core' (which fails on nuget.org) — only modules-overview.md §intro discloses the Sylin.Koan.* namespace. README's quickstart clone URLs point at github.com/koan-framework/* — a different org than sylin-org. module-ledger.md (the 'authoritative ledger') counts 65 modules vs 110 actual and contains duplicated, internally-contradictory sections (Koan.Storage/Web blocks repeated verbatim at lines 242-339 and 337-429).
tensions.evidence: README.md badges + install commands + clone URLs; version.json/NBGV vs docs front-matter 'framework_version: v0.6.3'; docs/reference/modules-overview.md 'Sylin.Koan.*' tip; docs/architecture/module-ledger.md snapshot (65 modules, 2025-09-29) + duplicate blocks

tensions.tension: principles.md — the document titled 'Architecture Principles' and linked from README as the 'Enterprise Architecture Guide' — illustrates the framework with substantially fictional APIs: IDataProvider/IDataProviderV2 (no such interface in src; the capability model is the actual surface per ARCH-0084), KoanEnv.Features.EnableExperimentalFeature (doesn't exist), Todo.SaveBatch (doesn't exist), AddKoan(options) (doesn't exist), capabilities.Has(DataCaps.Query.Linq) shown alongside the deleted-generation provider pattern. The canon document predates the consolidation and was never reconciled — violating the consolidation discipline's own rule that 'the facet's docs/guides are reconciled in the same breath (so docs never re-drift)' (plan §6.5), which was applied to capability guides but not to the philosophy doc itself.
tensions.evidence: principles.md (Provider Pattern, Version Compatibility, Telemetry, Batch Operations sections) vs grep results (0 hits for IDataProvider/IDataProviderV2/KoanEnv.Features/SaveBatch in src); ARCH-0084; foundation-consolidation-plan.md §6

tensions.tension: Provider transparency vs capability-graded divergence: 'same entity code works everywhere' is the promise, but the settled design deliberately makes behavior provider-dependent — TTL retention only on Mongo ([Index(Ttl=true)] skipped by relational adapters with periodic purge as backstop), conditional CAS claim only on SQLite/PG/SqlServer/Mongo, LINQ pushdown vs in-memory fallback, distributed transactions 'eventual consistency via Flow' (comparison.md scores itself 🟨 here). This is an honest, inherent tension the framework handles better than its competitors (fail-loud negotiation, capability ladder with constant at-least-once contract) — but the front-door docs state the promise without the grading, and only CLAUDE.md/ADRs state the grading.
tensions.evidence: principles.md 'Storage backend becomes a deployment concern'; CLAUDE.md JOBS-0005 §20 entries (capability-graded TTL/CAS); comparison.md Distributed-transactions row; ARCH-0084 capability negotiation

tensions.tension: 'Integration tests as canon — every adapter, connector, and pillar core ships an integration spec through real AddKoan()' (ARCH-0079, stated as universal) vs the ~25 zero-consumer experimental projects (AI verticals, Rag, Secrets, ServiceMesh, Recipe, Canon...) that have no such coverage and no consumers to drive it. The canon is real for the inner ring (per-connector *Capabilities.Spec.cs, convergence oracle, KoanIntegrationHost) and aspirational for the legacy/experimental strata — the rule's scope silently matches the redesign frontier, not the repo.
tensions.evidence: CLAUDE.md ARCH-0079 statement; ARCH-0084 §3 (conformance net exists for data/vector connectors); Stage 1 finding of ~25 zero-consumer projects; tests/Suites coverage concentrated on inner-ring pillars

tensions.tension: Founding 'No manual service configuration' vs the long-lived auto-vs-explicit split brain (~30 Add* extension methods beside 85 auto-registrars, plan §3 target #2). ARCH-0086's research substantially dissolved this — 'the Add* methods are mostly the registrar's own delegate... the good pattern, not duplication' — and healed the genuine split-brains (IKoanInitializer fold, auth off-registry AppDomain scan, GraphQl double-registration). Residual: 85 registrars remain on the legacy interface with conversion 'opportunistic', so two authoring surfaces coexist indefinitely by design.
tensions.evidence: foundation-consolidation-plan.md §3 #2 + §3a; ARCH-0086 Context + Consequences ('Two surfaces coexist during migration'); principles.md Auto-Registration Pattern

audience: The docs name at least four distinct personas, and they pull apart. (1) README's masthead and 2-minute pitch targets the individual/indie developer and 'small teams' — 'Try it. Be delighted'; AR ergonomics, zero config, SQLite-out-of-the-box. (2) enterprise-adoption.md targets architecture governance in an organization: pilots with 'leadership sponsorship', steering teams, KPIs, 'a guild of Koan champions', compliance mapping, data-residency adapters — an apparatus that presumes an installed base. (3) principles.md Strategic Direction explicitly targets 'experienced teams willing to invest in framework-specific expertise for long-term productivity gains' — the opposite onboarding posture from README's instant delight. (4) The consolidation-era ADRs declare the real premium-DX audience to be the adapter author ('samples never touch these types', ARCH-0084 Forces #1). The personas are not inherently incompatible — Rails serves indie and enterprise — but here they conflict in practice: the README persona is blocked at step one (package IDs are Sylin.Koan.*, public NuGet stale at 0.8.x, quickstart clone URLs point to a nonexistent koan-framework org), the enterprise persona is offered governance theater for a framework with zero external consumers and a falsely-versioned doc set, and the only persona actually served today is the fifth, undocumented one: the sole implementor-consumer dogfooding toward v1 (foundation-consolidation-plan.md §0 states this candidly). The consolidation plan is the only document whose stated audience matches reality; the public-facing docs are written for audiences that cannot yet onboard.
visionDebt: Flow event hooks: Flow.OnUpdate<T>(...) with UpdateResult.Continue() is showcased in README §4, overview.md Stage 3, and enterprise-adoption.md's compliance example — zero implementations or usages in any .cs file repo-wide. The entire 'event-driven architecture through simple patterns' pitch rides on it.

Opt-in telemetry API: AddKoan(options => options.EnableTelemetry = true) (principles.md) — AddKoan() has no options overload (src/Koan.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:23); OTel packages are unconditional Koan.Core dependencies.

principles.md's framework-evolution story: IDataProvider/IDataProviderV2 versioned interfaces and KoanEnv.Features.EnableExperimentalFeature feature flags — none exist; the capability model (ARCH-0084) is the real mechanism and principles.md doesn't describe it.

Escape-hatch surface as documented: User.ExecuteSql(...) and Todo.SaveBatch(...) don't exist under those names; Koan.Data.Direct (DirectSession) has zero consumers including dogfood — failing the plan's own 'a part earns its place' test.

Onboarding path: 'dotnet add package Koan.Core' fails on nuget.org (IDs are Sylin.Koan.*, stale 0.8.x vs source 0.17.x); README clone targets github.com/koan-framework/{quickstart,enterprise-sample} — wrong org (sylin-org), presumably dead links. The '2 minutes to working API' promise is unexecutable by an outsider today.

'koan export compose' deployment-artifact generation (README §5, enterprise-adoption pilot step, overview Stage 4) rides the ARCH-0077-condemned Orchestration CLI stack; 'Orchestration: Docker, Podman, Aspire, CLI automation' (README tech stack) likewise.

Enterprise governance apparatus (enterprise-adoption.md): pilot KPIs, champions guild, architecture review checklists — pure aspiration with no external consumer, no public versioned release line, and doc front-matter frozen at v0.6.3 since early 2026.

modules-overview.md sells pillars Stage 1 found to be zero-consumer or pre-renovation as production guidance: Recipe ('accelerating solution delivery'), Canon ('canonical projections, lineage'), Secrets, ServiceMesh-adjacent Inbox connector; and its Jobs entry describes the pre-JOBS-0005 'IJob + job runner' world that no longer exists.

'70+ integrated modules' (README) vs module-ledger's 65 (stale, 2025-09, with duplicated sections) vs 110 actual src projects — no document states the true module count or the inner-ring/experimental distinction the consolidation plan operates on.

Semantic streaming pipeline claims partially: .Pipeline()/Tokenize/Embed DSL exists (Koan.Core/Koan.AI Pipeline*Extensions), but README's claim it stores 'documents (PostgreSQL) + vectors (Weaviate) in one .Save()' with 'full observability' oversells the pre-renovation Messaging/Flow strata it composes with.
undersold: The unified capability model (ARCH-0084) — the framework's most distinctive settled achievement: one declare/negotiate/report primitive across 14 data+vector adapters, fail-loud CapabilityNotSupportedException, per-pillar token catalogs honoring Reference=Intent, /.well-known self-report. README mentions only a generic 'capability detection / fallback' table row; principles.md still shows the deleted generation.

Build-time source-generated discovery: ARCH-0086 reveals discovery is a Roslyn-generated [ModuleInitializer] registry (KoanRegistry) with topological [Before]/[After] ordering — AOT-friendly, deterministic, fast-boot — not runtime reflection magic. This directly answers the #1 enterprise objection to auto-registration frameworks, and no public-facing doc says it.

The Jobs pillar (JOBS-0005): entity-first IKoanJob<TSelf> with a capability ladder (in-memory → durable ledger → distributed competing-consumers → +bus) under a constant at-least-once + idempotent contract, contention-free CAS claim via a reusable IConditionalWriteRepository primitive, store-native TTL retention, cron/@boot/@continuous scheduling. Documented only in CLAUDE.md and the ADR; modules-overview.md actively misdescribes it.

Transparent entity caching (ARCH-0075/0078): [Cacheable(300)] L1/L2 with cross-node coherence channels, fresh-or-null read contract, opt-in-only SWR, per-request NoCache() — a principled answer to cache staleness that most frameworks fumble. Absent from README/overview entirely.

The engineering discipline itself: green ratchet (build + samples + doc-lint gate), cross-adapter filter convergence oracle that found real adapter bugs, ARCH-0079 integration-specs-through-real-AddKoan, and evidence-corrected ADRs (ARCH-0086 publicly reverses its own plan's premise on granularity findings). For an evaluator, this correctness culture is a stronger trust signal than any feature list, and it is invisible outside docs/decisions/.

Entity quality-of-life surface: GUID v7 auto-ids, [Timestamp]/[Timestamp(OnSave)], Copy/Move/Mirror cross-context transfer DSL, partition-name validation, [Index] — present in CLAUDE.md's utility catalog, absent from getting-started docs.

Security.Trust (fleet identity fabric direction) and the settled Web nucleus + EntityController payload-transformer/GraphQL/Swagger surface — Stage 1 found these settled or strategically coherent; the front door still sells the legacy Auth-flows stratum instead.

The honest multi-provider story: capability-graded behavior (TTL only where native, CAS where supported, pushdown-or-fail-loud) is a more credible pitch to senior engineers than the absolute 'same code works everywhere' claim the docs actually make — the framework undersells itself by overclaiming.
